stem: Square A has side lengths that are 247 times the side lengths of square B. The area of square A is k times the area of square B. What is the value of k?
rationale: The correct answer is 61009. For similar figures, the ratio of areas equals the square of the ratio of linear dimensions. Since the side-length ratio is 247, the area ratio is 247² = 61,009. So k = 61,009.
